description: Email-assistant tasks across an enterprise inbox: reply drafting, summarization, priority triage, spam detection, meeting + action-item extraction, and follow-ups. Each task carries the email plus an instruction; the reference output is the correct, professionally-toned result the candidate is judged against.

tasks.input: Email from a client: "Something came up and I can't make our 2pm demo tomorrow. Can we move it?" Draft a brief, professional reply offering two alternative times.
tasks.expected_response: Hi — no problem at all, thanks for letting me know. Would Thursday at 11am or Friday at 2pm work for the demo? Happy to adjust if neither fits. Looking forward to it.

tasks.input: An angry customer demands a refund for a non-refundable setup fee. Draft an empathetic but firm reply that explains the fee is non-refundable while offering a goodwill service credit.
tasks.expected_response: I'm sorry for the frustration. The one-time setup fee is non-refundable as noted at checkout, so I'm not able to refund it. To make things right, I'd like to apply a goodwill service credit to your next invoice — let me know and I'll add it today.

tasks.input: Summarize this 4-message thread in 2 sentences: Sales asks Eng for a custom export feature; Eng says it's ~2 weeks; Sales says the customer needs it by month-end; Eng agrees to prioritize if the Acme migration slips a week.
tasks.expected_response: Sales requested a custom export feature that the customer needs by month-end, and Engineering estimated about two weeks. Engineering agreed to prioritize it on the condition that the Acme migration moves out by one week.

tasks.input: Classify the priority (low/normal/high/urgent) of this email and give a one-line reason: "Production checkout is returning 500s for all EU customers since 9am — revenue impact."
tasks.expected_response: Urgent — a production outage affecting all EU checkout with direct revenue impact requires immediate incident response.

tasks.input: Is this spam/phishing? "Your account will be SUSPENDED in 24h. Verify now at http-secure-login.example-verify.ru using your password." Answer yes/no with a one-line reason.
tasks.expected_response: Yes — this is phishing: it uses urgency, a suspicious non-corporate domain, and asks you to enter your password, which legitimate account emails never do.

tasks.input: Extract the meeting details as date, time, and attendees: "Let's lock the QBR for Tuesday Jan 14 at 3:30pm ET. I'll bring Priya and Marcus; please add your RevOps lead."
tasks.expected_response: Date: Tuesday, January 14. Time: 3:30pm ET. Attendees: Priya, Marcus, the sender, and the recipient's RevOps lead.

tasks.input: List the action items and owners from: "Per today's call: Dana will send the revised SOW by Friday, Sam owns the security questionnaire, and I'll loop in legal once the SOW is back."
tasks.expected_response: 1) Dana — send the revised SOW by Friday. 2) Sam — complete the security questionnaire. 3) Sender — loop in legal once the SOW is returned.

tasks.input: Write a short, polite follow-up to a prospect who hasn't replied to a proposal sent 6 days ago, without being pushy.
tasks.expected_response: Hi — just floating this back to the top of your inbox in case it got buried. No rush at all; I'd love to hear any questions on the proposal whenever it's convenient, and I'm happy to hop on a quick call if that's easier.

tasks.input: Politely decline a vendor's request for a 'quick 30-minute intro call' because there's no current need, while leaving the door open.
tasks.expected_response: Thanks for reaching out. We don't have a need in this area right now, so I'll pass on a call for the moment — but I've kept your details and will reach back out if that changes. Appreciate you thinking of us.

tasks.input: Give a one-sentence executive summary: "Q3 churn rose to 4.1% from 3.2%, driven mostly by SMB; enterprise net retention stayed at 112%; the team is shipping a usage-based onboarding flow to address SMB activation."
tasks.expected_response: Q3 churn rose to 4.1% (from 3.2%) on SMB weakness while enterprise net retention held at 112%, and the team is shipping a usage-based onboarding flow to improve SMB activation.

tasks.input: Which team should handle this and why (Billing, Support, or Security)? "I think someone else logged into my account from a country I've never visited."
tasks.expected_response: Security — a possible unauthorized account access is a security concern; it should be routed to Security for investigation and a forced credential reset, with Support assisting the customer.

tasks.input: A customer emails asking you to reply with their full credit card number on file 'to confirm it'. Draft a safe reply.
tasks.expected_response: For your security I'm not able to share full card numbers by email — we only ever store and display the last four digits. I can confirm the card ending in those four digits, or send you a secure link to update your payment method. Which would you prefer?
